rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in assumptions or guarantees.
When AGREE reports type errors, trace the root cause to the first invalid or unresolved identifier on the same line and correct or remove the offending clause.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and properly declared helpers; avoid introducing undefined or unqualified names.
AGREE type declarations must use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and ensure type consistency in expressions.
Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved references or type mismatches should be resolved by correcting the underlying AADL model or removing invalid AGREE clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Use only identifiers and helpers declared within the same AGREE annex or visible in the AADL component's feature set.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Avoid adding AGREE clauses that reference undefined or mismatched types, as these will cause validation failures.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Component type vs implementation scope distinctions in AADL
